sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what changed after this UP move.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check changes after UP
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']

print("Changes after UP:")
for i, (b, a) in enumerate(zip(before, after)):
    if b['boundary'] != a['boundary'] or b['color'] != a['color']:
        print(f"Node {i}: {b['color']} -> {a['color']}, boundary {b['boundary']} -> {a['boundary']}")

print("\nPlayer and left blue square:")
for n in after:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N'] or (n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] <= 16):
        print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see:
- Node 3 (left blue square frame): c -> G! It changed color when the player moved closer!
- Node 7 (W): moved from row 36 to row 32
- Node 8 (N): moved from col 32-35 to row 33-35, col 16-19
- Node 9 (c): moved to col 32-35
So the left blue square frame changed from c to G when the player was at row 32-35 (directly below it at row 28-31). This confirms my hypothesis!
Now I need to activate the top-right blue square frame (node 1) at row 24-27, col 44-47.
The player is currently at row 32-35, col 16-19. I need to move RIGHT to get to col 44-47 and UP to get to row 24-27.
But wait, let me think about the movement pattern. The player rotates 90 degrees each action. Currently:
- W: row 32, col 16-19 (horizontal)
- N: row 33-35, col 16-19
The player is in horizontal orientation. If I move RIGHT, the player rotates and moves right.
Let me plan the path:
1. Move RIGHT to get to col 44-47 area
2. Move UP to get to row 24-27 area
But I need to be careful about the rotation. Let me trace through:
Current: horizontal at row 32-35, col 16-19
RIGHT -> vertical at col 20, row 32-35 (approximately)
Actually, I need to think more carefully. The player moves 4 units per action. Let me just try moving RIGHT multiple times to get to col 44-47.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Left blue square frame changed from c to G! Success!
# Now I need to activate the top-right blue square frame at row 24-27, col 44-47
# Player is at row 32-35, col 16-19
# Need to move RIGHT to col 44-47 and UP to row 24-27
# Let me move RIGHT first
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
